This digest highlights numerous breaking API changes, security enhancements, and significant updates to core browser components and UI.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public API for creating GPU contexts, such as viz::ContextProviderCommandBuffer::CreateForRaster, has been changed. The lose_context_when_out_of_memory parameter was removed, and embedders must update their calls. (b7eadee)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The net::URLRequestJob public interface has been updated. Methods like GetTotalReceivedBytes now return the type-safe base::ByteSize instead of int64_t. Embedders with custom protocol handlers must update their method signatures. (72bf5dc)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The searchbox.mojom interface was refactored to use a factory pattern. Consumers must now bind a searchbox::mojom::PageHandlerFactory instead of directly binding the PageHandler, breaking custom WebUI implementations. (a43ea83)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The ModelBrokerClient API in Optimization Guide now uses string-based use_case identifiers instead of a mojom::OnDeviceFeature enum. Embedders using on-device models must update their code. (dfe0d80)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The base::task::sequence_manager::ThreadControllerImpl class and its factory function CreateSequenceManagerOnCurrentThread have been removed. Embedders must migrate to CreateSequenceManagerOnCurrentThreadWithPump. (36e7905)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The recently added content::CdmHelper public interface and its header content/public/browser/cdm_helper.h have been reverted and removed completely. (7b96ee6)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public constructor for media::EncryptionPattern is now private and has been replaced by a static Create() factory method that returns an std::optional. Direct instantiation will no longer compile. (006ccbd)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The legacy ManagePasswordsIconView class and its header have been removed as part of a UI migration. Code referencing these components will fail to build. (822db52)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING In the updater's PolicyService, the public function AreUpdatesSuppressedNow has been renamed to AreUpdatesSuppressed and its signature was changed to require an explicit base::Time parameter. (d166a73)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The AccessibilityAnnotatorBackend::Observer public interface was modified. The OnContentAnnotationsAdded method now requires a const ContentAnnotationsData& parameter, breaking existing observer implementations. (fd9d5b4)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The FetchEmailOneTimeTokenResponse protobuf message in the One-Time Token Service API has been changed, removing the created_timestamp and received_timestamp fields. (4ce640a)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The legacy GlicFreUI has been deleted, along with its public headers such as glic_fre_ui.h. Any code referencing this UI will no longer compile. (dc4535b)

Browser & Core APIs

  • The BrowserContext::UniqueId() method is now deprecated. A new BrowserContext::UniqueToken() method returning a more secure base::UnguessableToken should be used instead. (4f81ffc) (f2112b5)
  • Asynchronous methods have been added to PageContentExtractionService, allowing callers to wait for pending page analysis results. (691d55a)
  • A new UnexportableSigningKeyId type was introduced to provide stronger type safety for hardware-backed signing keys. (a2bcfb9)
  • On Windows, a DCHECK failure in VideoCaptureDeviceFactoryWin was fixed by preventing unnecessary restarts of the COM thread, improving stability. (2edfa92)
  • The WebCrypto API now supports ML-KEM-768 and ML-KEM-1024 for post-quantum key encapsulation. (b582009)

Security

  • A Use-After-Free vulnerability in WebContentsImpl::ForSecurityDropFullscreen was fixed by using WeakPtr to safely handle WebContents instances during nested message loops. (899e77f)
  • A re-entrancy bug in TabStripModel that could cause a Use-After-Free during tab move operations has been fixed. (0985a47)
  • For FedCM, a check was added to validate the initiator's origin when processing Set-Login headers in the browser process, enhancing security for subresource loads. (5932bfd)
  • The XKB keyboard layout name parser was hardened to prevent potential path traversal attacks. (73daddb)
  • Integer overflow vulnerabilities in the H.264 parser were fixed by using checked arithmetic. (4986289)
  • A Use-After-Free in the DevTools agent host was patched by preventing double initialization. (5b7fd82)

UI & Browser Features

  • The "Send Tab to Self" feature will now display a toast notification on desktop platforms when a tab is automatically opened. (eecf3ec)
  • The updater UI on Windows now supports high DPI scaling, resulting in a sharper interface. (c154701)
  • The "Default Search Engine Reset" dialog has been refactored to use ui::DialogModel, updating its text and converting the "Learn more" button into an inline link. (6f48ee3)
  • A dangling pointer crash in the bookmark context menu on macOS was resolved by converting a raw_ptr to a WeakPtr. (c31e869)
  • The Task Manager was updated to correctly display prerendered new tabs by adding a new PrerenderNewTabTag. (7c4cbb7)
  • A new enterprise policy has been added to allow overriding the CPU Performance API. (1bff823)

Extensions

  • A crash in the OneTimeMessageHandler has been fixed. The handler now validates that the ScriptContext is still active before processing a message response, preventing a use-after-free. (94a8ea5)
  • A crash in ExtensionSyncService was fixed by adding a null check when retrieving granted permissions, preventing issues with incomplete sync data. (0eeafe7)
  • Content Security Policy (CSP) enforcement was hardened for sandboxed extension service workers. (a0b8451)

Networking

  • The WebSocket implementation over HTTP/3 was enhanced by implementing GetLoadTimingInfo, GetSSLInfo, and GetRemoteEndpoint in the handshake stream. (9a70b09)

Build & Infrastructure

  • The enable_surface_embed build flag has been removed, making the feature's code always available when WebContents is used and simplifying build configurations. (b27035e)
  • On Windows, usage of the Active Template Library (ATL) has been removed from content_main.cc as part of a larger effort to remove ATL from the codebase. (fc1e63c)
  • Numerous components continue to be modularized to improve build structure and dependency hygiene, including push_messaging, nearby_sharing, and headless browser mode. (da19370) (37803b9) (b4167ed)

2026-04-09

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-09

Overview

This daily summary for April 9, 2026 covers 528 relevant commits out of a total of 721 on the main branch. A significant number of breaking changes were landed, primarily focused on refactoring core browser APIs, removing legacy functionality, and cleaning up data structures. The first relevant commit was (f3cfa32) and the last was (1b28d58).

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The widely used public functions chrome::FindBrowserWithWindow and chrome::FindBrowserWithID have been removed. Embedders must update their code to use the new GlobalBrowserCollection singleton, for example: GlobalBrowserCollection::GetInstance()->FindBrowserWithWindow(). The return type for the window lookup has also changed from Browser* to BrowserWindowInterface*, requiring further refactoring. (2a4a86d) (fb39bae)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The integer values of the public media::Channels enum in media/base/channel_layout.h have been changed to align with the WAVEFORMATEXTENSIBLE standard. Any code that relies on the specific numeric values of these channels for audio processing will break. (898ad6b)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING A new pure virtual method, NotifySwappedRWHVChildFrameFromRenderManager, has been added to the content::RenderFrameHostManager::Delegate interface. Custom implementations of this delegate must now provide an implementation for this method to avoid build failures. (b83f5c5)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING In the base threading library, Thread::Delegate::AddTaskObserver and the corresponding task_observer option have been removed. It is no longer possible to add a TaskObserver upon thread creation. (e501d62)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The ocsp_result field has been removed from the public net::SSLInfo struct as part of the deprecation of the Expect-Staple feature. Code inspecting OCSP verification results via this field will no longer compile. (f366471)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The FontService::ListFamilies() method has been removed from the public font_service.mojom interface. Clients using this IPC to enumerate system fonts will be broken. (bb555c3)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING On Windows, the public methods Emf::InitFromFile() and Emf::InitToFileForTesting() have been removed from the exported printing::Emf class. (2abeb2f)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The rule_options member has been removed from the content_settings::RuleMetaData struct and its corresponding mojom definition, breaking any code that reads or writes to it. (e8b22be)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public operator== has been removed from the passage_embeddings::Embedding and history_embeddings::UrlData data structures, which will break tests or other code relying on direct equality comparison. (1aa8ca6)

API & Core Infrastructure

  • The TabGroup class in components/tabs now has an observer pattern, allowing clients to subscribe directly to group and visual data changes, simplifying UI updates. (9236242) (e74b83d)
  • As part of a large-scale refactoring, many components like ChromeTracingDelegate and SmartRestartMetrics were migrated away from the legacy BrowserList to use the new GlobalBrowserCollection and its observer. (6f905a1) (a9834f7) (59169c4)
  • Several components were updated from the legacy MemoryPressureListener to the new MemoryConsumer API for improved memory management. (5bf4747)
  • On Windows, dependencies on the legacy ATL framework were removed from UI automation, accessibility event waiters, and the updater in favor of modern WRL and base library equivalents. (c434495) (f3cfa32)
  • A new BrowserLaunchEvent proto was added to the enterprise reporting pipeline to capture browser launch information. (c6c9731)

Security & Privacy

  • To prevent a compromised renderer from spoofing audio activity, the browser process now verifies that a renderer is authorized to bypass the browser's audio service before marking a tab as 'audible'. (c1f3553)
  • A same-origin check is now enforced for main script fetches in Dedicated Workers to prevent a compromised renderer from exfiltrating assets from other origins. This is controlled by the kEnforceDedicatedWorkerSameOriginCheck feature flag. (7d3c950)
  • On Windows, a potential local privilege escalation vulnerability in Chrome Remote Desktop was fixed by using user impersonation when writing WebAuthn extension files. (98ca974)
  • For enterprise users, deep scanning for downloads now supports user-initiated cancellation. (d858c06)
  • The experimental ChallengeURL prototype was removed from the WebAuthn API as it was not on a standards track. (24b759e)

UI & Features

  • The WebUI home button now supports drag-and-drop for setting the homepage URL. (5bc04ab)
  • An implementation was added to automatically open tabs received via the Send-Tab-To-Self feature on desktop platforms, controlled by a new feature flag. (a77fda5)
  • On macOS, an issue causing the titlebar to get stuck in fullscreen when using vertical tabs has been resolved. (72c5f64)
  • Accessibility for the password generation popup has been improved for screen reader users by ensuring the popup widget is activated, allowing for proper navigation. (939b475)
  • The Windows updater UI has been polished with a smoother, anti-aliased progress bar and adjusted button colors. (64c765f) (79e3c49)

Testing & Tooling

  • Chrome for Testing (CfT) now supports a runtime configuration JSON file, specified by the --chrome-for-testing-config switch. The initial implementation adds an option to enable a virtual clipboard. (bca0df9)
  • The eventToPromise() test utility in WebUI has been updated to be strongly typed with TypeScript, improving test code quality and maintainability. (adadafb) (49febf0)
  • The Extreme Lightweight UMA Detector (ELUD) now embeds object and allocation sizes into its zap pattern, aiding in the diagnosis of memory corruption bugs from crash reports. (cbcea52)

2026-04-08

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-08

Overview

This summary covers 793 commits on the main branch, with 622 identified as relevant to developers working on Chromium-based projects. The changes span from commit (fea075a) to (1572e92). This was an exceptionally active day for API refactoring, resulting in a large number of breaking changes across core utilities, browser interfaces, and networking. Other notable changes include ongoing security hardening and UI refinements to context menus and side panels.

Breaking Changes: Core APIs & Interfaces

  • ⚠️ BREAKING base::ObserverList now disallows re-entrant iteration by default on desktop platforms. The default policy has been changed from kAllowReentrancy to kDisallowReentrancy. Code that relies on nested observer notifications will now trigger DCHECK failures and must be updated to explicitly use the kAllowReentrancy policy. (83dc1d0) (cf7e006)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The BrowserWindow public interface has been significantly refactored. The pure virtual method ZoomChangedForActiveTab() has been removed, and numerous utility functions in signin, sync, and web_applications now accept a BrowserWindowInterface* instead of a Browser*. Custom BrowserWindow implementations must be updated. (0501de8) (c7fb45c)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The method for querying browser instances has been changed. The free functions chrome::GetTotalBrowserCount() and chrome::GetBrowserCount() have been removed. Callers must migrate to the new GlobalBrowserCollection and ProfileBrowserCollection APIs. (ebcb57e)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The extensions::RuntimeAPIDelegate::OpenOptionsPage method is now asynchronous. Its signature has changed from returning a bool to returning void and taking a base::OnceCallback<void(bool)>. Embedders with custom implementations must update to the new signature. (011e693)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The content::RenderWidgetHostView public interface has a new pure virtual method, HasSavedFrame(). Embedders with custom implementations of this interface must add an implementation for this new method to avoid compilation errors. (1e42bd2)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public virtual method ContentBrowserClient::IsIsolatedContextAllowedForUrl has been removed. Embedders must now implement the new DirectSocketsDelegate::AreDirectSocketsAllowed to grant access to APIs like Direct Sockets. (5697236)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public constructor for AuthenticatorWin now requires a gfx::NativeWindow. Code that relied on the previous constructor, which automatically found the active browser window, must be updated to explicitly provide the native window handle. (3df2ead)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The RecordReplayDriver class has been refactored into a pure abstract interface. Embedders directly using the old concrete class must update their code to use the new ContentRecordReplayDriver implementation and associated factory patterns. (560f67a)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The static method TranslateLanguageList::DisableUpdate() has been removed. Tests that used this method to prevent server-side language list updates must be updated to use the new constructor that allows injecting a mock TranslateUrlFetcher. (6aef37c)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Multiple base and content APIs have been changed. This includes replacing std::map with base::FieldTrialParams in feature list APIs, removing GetIgnoredDuplicateNavigationCount() from content::NavigationHandle, and refactoring base::trait_helpers to use base::ParameterPack. (9aa846e) (2950468) (0b91f88)

Breaking Changes: Networking & Mojo

  • ⚠️ BREAKING Significant Third-Party Cookie Deprecation (3PCD) logic has been removed from the network stack. This includes the removal of the kTpcdMetadataStageControl and kTpcdMetadataGrants features, TPCD_METADATA and TPCD_HEURISTICS content setting types, and related DevTools parameters. Embedders relying on these for 3PCD integration will need to adapt. (708fe41)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING APIs for secure cookie exemptions have been migrated from scheme/host-based to origin-based. This changes method signatures in content_settings::CookieSettings and net::CookieAccessDelegate, requiring embedders to update how they configure cookie exemptions. (1575beb)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The ContentSettingsManager::AllowStorageAccess mojo interface signature has been changed. The origin, site_for_cookies, and top_frame_origin parameters were removed, as this information is now derived from the RenderFrameHost on the browser side. (8c04824)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The chrome.mojom.PluginInfoHost.GetPluginInfo() Mojo interface has been changed. The origin input parameter was removed to prevent the renderer from supplying an incorrect origin. Consumers and implementers of this interface must update to the new signature. (3c9de1b)

Security

  • Multiple security vulnerabilities were addressed, including a Use-After-Free in PasswordFormManager, a state leak in the File System Access API, a UAF in WebSocketSpdyStreamAdapter, and a potential out-of-bounds write in the GPU command buffer. (c542194) (a4c6a71) (5d8f582) (dc5e20c)
  • WebAuthn "Immediate Mode" with passwords and passkeys has been enabled by default. (7b93b74)
  • A potential browser crash during printing has been fixed. The browser now safely rejects an invalid memory handle from the renderer instead of crashing. (7d72d49)

APIs & Extensions

  • The views::WebView class now includes a SetOwnedWebContents() method, allowing it to take ownership of an externally created WebContents instance. (a203cc3)
  • The PageContentExtractionService has new asynchronous methods (Get...Async) that wait for initial content extraction to complete, preventing race conditions on page load. (9bd9cbe)
  • Service worker registration for extensions will now be retried on a transient kErrorNotFound error, improving reliability in certain enterprise environments. (0c3687a)
  • The network::DataElement public API was enhanced with a TryAs<T> helper to simplify type checking and data access in a single operation. (09fd4dc)

Browser UI & Features

  • The "Send to your devices" option in context menus has been converted into a dynamic submenu that directly lists available target devices, controlled by the kSendTabToSelfShowTargetsInContextMenus feature. (1399633)
  • The "Tabs From Other Devices" side panel now has an entry point in the 3-dot menu and its availability is now restricted to regular profiles (excluding incognito and guest). (0964b4e) (33653e7)
  • On macOS, support for the AXTextOperation parameterized attribute was implemented, enabling bulk text operations like find-and-replace for accessibility clients. (dea0b52)
  • The TabStrip WebUI playground at chrome://tab-strip/, an early testing ground for the feature, has been removed as it is now obsolete. (5e4e689)

Testing & Infrastructure

  • ChromeDriver was optimized to avoid redundant focus script calls if an element is already active, which could previously cause issues with contenteditable elements. (a310a5c)
  • A large-scale refactoring migrated numerous keyed service factories from the Singleton pattern to the preferred NoDestructor pattern for improved memory management and shutdown behavior. (6d036b4)

2026-04-07

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-07

Overview

On April 7, 2026, the Chromium main branch saw 582 commits, with 502 identified as relevant to developers. This day was marked by a large number of significant breaking changes, primarily driven by the 'Project Bedrock' refactoring which replaces Browser* pointers with a new BrowserWindowInterface. Other major breaks include the removal of third-party cookie deprecation APIs, changes to core networking interfaces, and an upgrade of the TypeScript compiler. The first commit of the day was (e1a3d4f) and the last was (5a63e24).

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The 'Project Bedrock' refactoring continues, replacing raw Browser* pointers with the narrower BrowserWindowInterface* in many public APIs. This affects core utilities like chrome::FindBrowserWithTab, session service helpers in session_service_lookup.h, and UI coordinators like CastDialogCoordinator. Embedders must update call sites to handle the new interface. (78da6a2) (d9ac938) (05f77bc)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Public getters on BrowserWindowFeatures for accessing UI controllers like LensOverlayEntryPointController and TabSearchToolbarButtonController have been removed. Access must now be done via the static ::From(browser) method on the respective controller classes. (6d74b29) (a89d948)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The ui::views::BubbleAnchor type has been refactored from a variant alias into a dedicated class. This changes the public API for views::BubbleDialogDelegateView and other components that create UI bubbles, requiring significant updates to bubble creation code. (1ba6a69)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Core networking APIs have changed. Methods in net::HttpTransaction that return byte counts now use the net::ByteSize class instead of int64_t. Additionally, the Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) API on net::URLRequest was changed from set_allows_device_bound_sessions(bool) to set_device_bound_session_mode(net::DeviceBoundSessionMode). (79bbb97) (b057ca4)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING As part of the ongoing third-party cookie deprecation (3PCD) cleanup, the TpcdMetadataManager and its factory have been deleted entirely. Furthermore, the public method net::CookieSettingsBase::IsExemptFromThirdPartyCookieBlocking and related exemption reasons have been removed. (b7f05fa) (c9934fd)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING For security hardening, the extension downloader will no longer follow HTTP redirects when fetching CRX files. This is a major behavioral change that will break custom or enterprise extension update servers that rely on redirects. (04fad69)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The TypeScript compiler has been upgraded to v6.0.2, which deprecates older syntax and may require updates to custom WebUI code. The public testing utility PrerenderTestHelper::AddPrerenderUntilScriptAsync also had its signature changed. (83a142e) (e3d4fd8)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Several other public APIs were changed or removed: sql::Database::ReportMemoryUsage was deleted, the AutofillSuggestionDelegate interface was modified, the kBackgroundMediaCapture feature flag was moved out of the public content::features namespace, and the DataTypeStore::DeleteAllDataAndMetadata signature was updated. (b93f6d1) (5fe250d) (3ecbb63) (bbdfaaf)

Security

  • A Use-After-Free (UAF) vulnerability in ServiceWorkerCacheStorageMatcher was fixed by improving the lifetime management of the script sender. (d17fe11)
  • The Viz service now validates TextureQuad data during deserialization to harden the browser against malicious renderer processes. (acafd83)
  • A path traversal vulnerability on Windows was fixed in ChildProcessSecurityPolicyImpl to better handle parent directory references like ... (8f31946)
  • A one-minute grace period was added for Signed Certificate Timestamp (SCT) validity checking to better tolerate minor clock skew between clients and servers. (f7d2cdc)

Networking

  • The public net::ResolutionDetails struct was expanded to include task_completion_delay and secure_dns_attempted, providing more detailed information about DNS resolutions. (e3c1d6a)
  • A performance issue causing inefficient one-byte reads in HttpStreamParser has been fixed. (4168e93)
  • A bug was fixed in the Connection Allowlist feature to ensure violation reports are sent to the correct endpoint specified by the document's Reporting-Endpoint header. (5564461)

Browser UI & Features

  • The ui::menus::SimpleMenuModel API has been enhanced to support secondary labels, allowing for richer context menus. (a6fb10a)
  • A new context menu item has been added to allow pinning and unpinning the tab search button in the tab strip. (04d2971)
  • An entry point to show and pin the 'Tabs From Other Devices' side panel has been added to the 'Customize Chrome' side panel. (1f82a70)
  • The SyncShowBookmarksLimitExceededError feature, which displays an error when the bookmark storage limit is reached, has been enabled by default. (454badd)
  • The experimental TabStripExperimentService has been removed, with its functionality for updating tab group visuals consolidated into the main TabStripService. (792acce)

Core, Build & Testing

  • A new configuration system was added for Chrome for Testing (CfT), allowing runtime options to be specified via a JSON file with the --chrome-for-testing-config switch. (f908610)
  • A brief but significant breaking change to the allocator_shim::UncheckedAlignedAlloc function signature was introduced and quickly reverted due to build failures. (2b3ee80) (f747855)
  • On Windows, a new feature flag (RemoveGpuJobMemoryLimit) was added to optionally remove the job memory limit for the GPU process, aiding in memory usage measurement for features like WebGPU. (705baec)
  • Several components, including //chrome/browser/feed, //chrome/browser/performance_manager, and //chrome/browser/component_updater, were modularized into their own BUILD.gn files to improve dependency management. (bfec926) (879e523) (c44b7b1)

2026-04-06

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-06

Overview

On April 6th, 2026, 215 of 282 total commits were identified as relevant for developers building on Chromium for desktop platforms. This summary covers changes from commit (95b6d18) to (0c355e9), highlighting two significant breaking changes in the compositor and accessibility APIs, the introduction of a new TabStrip API, and notable updates to networking and security.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public cc::LayerTreeHostClient interface has been changed. The virtual method WillBeginImplCommit was removed, and its logic merged into WillCommit. Embedders with custom LayerTreeHostClient implementations must remove the WillBeginImplCommit override to avoid build failures. The cc::TextureLayer::Create factory method signature was also updated. (7010b87)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: A stability check has been promoted from a debug-only DCHECK to a production CHECK on Windows to prevent re-entrant calls to the public BrowserAccessibilityManager::OnAccessibilityEvents method. Embedders whose code triggers this condition will now experience a production crash, hardening the API but requiring potential code updates. (c52ae89)

Browser & UI

  • A new TabStrip API is being introduced to allow programmatic updates to the tab strip. Initial commits add the service definition and an Update method, starting with support for updating a TabGroup's visual data. (ea91004) (4a588dd)
  • A race condition causing a dangling pointer in BackgroundModeManager was fixed. The fix ensures old status icon menus are kept alive until the context menu update is fully complete, preventing crashes. (620a047)

Networking & Security

  • Detailed DNS resolution information (e.g., source of result like cache, DoH, or system resolver) is now propagated through the network stack. This data is available in LoadTimingInternalInfo and can be used for more granular performance analysis. (2e629dc) (3c5e3ba) (dd8d63c) (7288496)
  • A new feature flag, kIgnoreCorsPreflightPolicy, was added to the Network Service. When enabled, this flag will ignore the kPreventPreflight policy, moving towards its deprecation. (55b0c31)
  • A security fix prevents a potential user activation bypass. System-triggered media suspensions, such as audio focus loss, no longer grant a transient user activation token to the frame. (55823cd)
  • The experimental ServiceWorkerAutoPreload feature for subresources has been removed. This includes the removal of the kAutoPreload enum value from the public blink.mojom.ServiceWorkerFetchHandlerBypassOption interface. (2bb2c50)
  • Bug fixes for Service Workers were landed, addressing a potential use-after-free in ServiceWorkerVersion and a bug that caused requests to stall when a fetch event was restarted. (405dcf0) (bc87e02)
  • The list of bulk HSTS (HTTP Strict Transport Security) preloaded entries has been updated. (baeca77)

Extensions

  • Several internal enterprise extension APIs, including enterprise.platformKeys and enterprise.networkingAttributes, were migrated from JSON schema to WebIDL as part of an ongoing refactoring. This is not expected to impact extension behavior. (19c7e7f) (f610734) (3440252)
  • The EnterpriseExtensionDOMActivityTelemetry feature, which collects DOM activity for enterprise-installed extensions, has been enabled by default. Its runtime state is now tracked via a synthetic field trial for performance analysis. (1775f9a) (00f7844)

Testing & Infrastructure

  • A new InteractionTestUtilSimulatorWebUI was implemented to allow automated tests to interact with tracked WebUI elements using the same APIs as for Views and WebContents, improving testability of WebUI components. (3609c24)
  • As part of the ongoing modularization of //chrome/browser, source files related to downloads were moved into a new //chrome/browser/download component, breaking a dependency cycle. (38b45dc) (5adc86a)
  • A large-scale code health effort fixed "Include What You Use" (IWYU) violations related to base/logging.h across many files, improving build hygiene. (3be1f60)

2026-04-05

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-05

Overview

This digest covers 3 commits. After filtering for changes relevant to Windows and macOS desktop embedders, 2 commits were analyzed. The changes focus on stability improvements in window management and bug fixes within the payments component. No major breaking changes were identified.

UI & Window Management

  • A Use-After-Free vulnerability in TransientWindowManager has been resolved by replacing base::AutoReset with base::WeakAutoReset. This change prevents memory access after the window manager has been destroyed during synchronous callbacks (e.g., window_->Hide()), improving overall browser stability for desktop platforms. (ab2ec9c)

Payments

  • The PaymentLinkManager logic was updated to prevent its state from being modified while a payment link operation is already in progress. This avoids potential race conditions and ensures more predictable behavior for facilitated payments. (2071ecb)

2026-04-04

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-04

Overview

This digest covers 39 commits, with 12 analyzed after filtering for browser, UI, and extensions changes relevant to Windows and macOS embedders. Highlights include significant progress on a new WebUI-based toolbar, internal modernization of the extensions framework, and various UI feature enhancements. No major breaking changes were identified.

Browser UI & Features

  • The new WebUI-based toolbar continues to be built out, with new implementations for setting element identifiers (SetActionElementIdentifier), updating action states (UpdateActionState), and adding a visual divider. These changes enable toolbar actions to be used as UI anchors and support ephemeral states. (fcb45c1) (5b3d5f4) (3df9083)
  • A new 'What's New' page variant has been added, and metrics are now logged when users enable Vertical Tabs from this page. Visual updates were made to the Vertical Tabs combo button to improve usability on hover. Additionally, a use-after-free bug in the ToastController was fixed to improve stability. (851803b) (3759f15) (69a7fe2) (53245ff)

Extensions

  • Several enterprise-focused extension APIs, including enterprise.login, enterprise.kioskInput, enterprise.hardwarePlatform, and enterprise.deviceAttributes, have been converted internally from JSON schema definitions to WebIDL. This is part of an ongoing modernization effort and does not affect the public API contract for these extensions. (596b815) (09c68dd) (345d69d) (16b705d)
  • A stability fix was made to the extensions EventRouter to correctly handle malformed IPC messages, preventing a potential browser process crash. (ba05b12)

2026-04-03

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-03

Overview

This summary covers 340 relevant commits out of 463 total on the main branch, from commit (3622de3) to (1e376a2). The day saw a significant number of breaking API removals, particularly in identity management and UI components, alongside security hardening in the media pipeline and networking stack.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The signin::IdentityManagerProvider class and its helper functions have been removed. Embedders must now depend on IdentityManagerFactory directly to retrieve the IdentityManager for a given browser context. (4db6a2e)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Numerous public OAuth2 scope constants have been made private by moving them from google_apis/gaia/gaia_constants.h to an internal file. Projects that referenced these constants will no longer compile. (8b1ec69)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING In the Views framework, several deprecated accessibility methods like SetLiveStatus and SetLiveRelevant have been removed from ui::views::accessibility::ViewAccessibility. Code must be updated to use the new SetLiveRegionContainer() API. (2493686) (7d4d666)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public virtual method WebAppUiManager::ShowWebAppIdentityUpdateDialog and its associated views have been removed. This is part of a move to a new, predictable PWA identity update flow. (e05db21)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public methods set_is_secondary_widget(bool) and is_secondary_widget() have been removed from the core ui/views/widget/widget.h interface. (c61eb90)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The stable kWebUITabStrip feature has been completely removed. Builds or embedders that enabled this feature will be impacted. (880ccd9)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public function WorkingDirectoryIndependentServerNameFromUTF8 has been removed from the named_mojo_ipc_server component. (1e376a2)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The feature flag enable-windows-gaming-input-data-fetcher has been removed, enforcing the use of the Windows.Gaming.Input API and removing the XInput fallback. This may alter gamepad compatibility on Windows. (4aa5edf)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The unimplemented public method IsFocusable() has been removed from components/autofill/core/common/form_field_data.h, which will cause build failures for any code that called it. (9780ea5)

Security

  • ⚠️ BREAKING A security hardening measure now rejects EncryptionScheme::kUnencrypted during Mojo deserialization of media::DecryptConfig. This behavioral change prevents a compromised renderer from bypassing subsample validation and will cause previously permissible insecure requests to fail. (878ee24)
  • On Windows, the File System Access API now blocks write actions to paths matching */.git/hooks to mitigate a potential remote code execution vulnerability. (fb916b2) (5ada3cd)
  • The File System Manager now enforces the use of the browser-authoritative origin for security checks when opening a file system, ignoring any origin supplied by the renderer process. (809c967)
  • A vulnerability in the Direct Sockets API has been patched to prevent a UDP socket from being sent to a local multicast address without proper permission. (d3e49ac)
  • To prevent potential XS-Search attacks, LCP Critical Path Predictor (LCPP) hints are now cleared during redirects. (ef0bfae)

Browser UI & Features

  • A new feature, gated by the kSplitViewLinkOpen flag, adds a Ctrl+Alt+Click shortcut to open links in a split view. This introduces a new NEW_SPLIT_VIEW value to the WindowOpenDisposition enum. (7f076b4)
  • New end-to-end latency metrics have been added to the Send Tab To Self feature, including TimeSentToReceived and TimeSentToOpened. (fe3a70e)
  • A Use-After-Free crash during the transition to fullscreen on Windows has been fixed by adding WeakPtr guards in RenderFrameHostImpl and WebContentsImpl. (91d5baa)
  • A crash on macOS when disconnecting a vibrating Xbox controller has been fixed in the Gamepad service. (713f78c)

Networking

  • A behavioral change now enforces Content Security Policy (CSP) checks for <link rel="modulepreload"> tags that lack an as attribute, treating them as scripts. This may break sites that relied on the previous, more permissive behavior. (ec9209f)

Extensions

  • Event dispatching logic has been made more robust. The cannot_dispatch_callback will now be more reliably invoked when an event goes unhandled, particularly in edge cases involving lazy listeners. (6675d2e)

Autofill

  • Several feature flags for CVC (Card Verification Code) storage have been removed as the feature is now stable and enabled by default. This includes SyncAutofillWalletCredentialData and AutofillEnableCvcStorageAndFilling. (9249343)

Build System & Testing

  • The testing utility base::test::ScopedFeatureList is now move-constructible, making it easier to use in test fixture member initializer lists. (6a6b538)
  • Several browser components, including //chrome/browser/translate, //chrome/browser/speech, and //chrome/browser/collaboration, have been modularized into separate :impl and public header targets to improve dependency management. (10cff87) (b1d2c70) (340a3ca)

2026-04-02

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-02

Overview

This daily summary for the main branch on 2026-04-02 covers 522 relevant commits out of 699 total, from commit (6a50027) to (8c20471). The day was marked by an unusually high number of breaking changes to core APIs, including base::Thread, ServiceProcessHost, and browser window creation functions, requiring significant updates for embedders. Other key areas of focus include security hardening in DevTools and media handling, UI improvements for vertical tabs, and ongoing refactoring of enterprise and sign-in components.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The constructor for base::Thread has been overhauled. The simple Thread(const std::string& name) constructor is removed. Embedders must now use new signatures that explicitly pass a delegate or a Restartable tag, such as Thread(const std::string& name, std::unique_ptr<Delegate> delegate). (7d91034)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING As part of the "Bedrock" UI abstraction project, the return type of common functions like chrome::OpenEmptyWindow has changed from Browser* to BrowserWindowInterface*. Code calling these browser creation and command functions must be updated to handle the new interface. (4357394)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The global ServiceProcessHost::Observer interface has been removed. Embedders must now use the per-instance ServiceProcessHost::Options::WithObserver() pattern to monitor service process lifecycle events, as ServiceProcessHost::AddObserver() no longer exists. (ca7b044)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public method WebView::SetCrashedOverlayView has been removed from ui/views/controls/webview/webview.h. Embedders using this method to display a custom "sad tab" or crashed overlay view must migrate to the newer client-owned widget model. (b5d130c)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING New pure virtual functions have been added to the FilesRequestHandlerBase::Delegate interface in the enterprise connectors component. Existing implementations of this delegate will fail to compile until they implement the new methods (UpdateRequestHandlerResult, GetPath, etc.). (de1b75b)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING To fix a security vulnerability, the signature of content::MediaSessionPlayerObserver::OnResume has been changed from OnResume(int player_id) to OnResume(int player_id, bool triggered_by_user). All implementations of this observer interface must be updated. (75237fb)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Numerous public OAuth2 scope constants (e.g., kGetCheckConnectionInfoScope, kOAuth1LoginScope) have been removed from google_apis/gaia/gaia_constants.h. Embedders using these constants for sign-in integrations must update their code. (4fd8668)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The default trait for base::ScopedObservation has been changed to assume pointers are not dangling. This alters the default contract of this core utility and may cause runtime crashes in code that previously relied on the more lenient default behavior. (95eade2)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public method VerticalTabStripStateController::SetCollapsed(bool) has been made private. It is replaced by the new public method RequestCollapse(bool), which uses a delegate to manage the transition. (3407ab5)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public Blink API method WebString::FromUTF16() has been renamed to WebString::FromUtf16(). C++ code that creates Blink strings from UTF-16 will fail to compile until updated. (cf3132f)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The structure blink::RecordContentToVisibleTimeRequest has been refactored. Boolean flags have been replaced with a std::vector of blink::VisibleTimeEvent structs, requiring updates for embedders using this performance measurement API. (a71337a)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public method ShouldShowAnchoredMessageCloseIcon has been removed from the PageActionController interface, replaced by the more generic SetAnchoredMessageAction method. (69a05e6)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public enum toasts::ToastDismissMenuEntries has been removed from chrome/browser/ui/toasts/toast_metrics.h. (79bec11)

Security

  • A DevTools vulnerability where a compromised renderer could hijack sessions via Fenced Frames has been mitigated by adding global uniqueness checks for DevTools tokens. (a77ddcd)
  • To prevent cross-origin information leaks, the Service Worker Static Routing API will now block opaque responses for navigation requests that match a cache source. This is gated by the kServiceWorkerStaticRouterOpaqueCheck feature. (1cd1e86)
  • A potential integer overflow vulnerability in the GPU command buffer was fixed by adding checked arithmetic and size validation. (39eb06c)
  • Security for HLS (HTTP Live Streaming) was enhanced to restrict key loading from insecure cross-origin sources unless proper CORS headers are present. (db46e35)
  • The internal data structures for static HSTS and PKP have been separated to improve maintainability and remove confusing edge cases. (8119b58)

Browser UI & Features

  • Numerous improvements were made to the experimental Vertical Tabs feature, including fixing a drag-and-drop image lag on macOS, ensuring the UI repaints on theme changes, and preventing the expand-on-hover state from conflicting with an open Omnibox. (5c294f0) (276d08e) (5df2bb9) (2403cb4) (6658c0f)
  • Initial scaffolding was added for a new "Tabs from other devices" side panel. The feature is not yet functional. (b0e4272)
  • The user-visible string "Connected Accounts" was changed to "Linked Accounts" throughout the sign-in UI. (ddae6ea)
  • On Windows, a change that attempted to respect autocorrect="off" was reverted, as it caused text to be overwritten when using an IME. (2f62cf5)
  • The kUseLastVisitedFallbackURLFavicon feature, which improves favicon handling for history entries, has been enabled by default. (ef93dd8)

APIs & Core Internals

  • Several enterprise-related functions and tests for deep scanning and crash key management have been moved from //chrome to the more central //components/enterprise/connectors location to improve modularity. (f8ab016) (baab27a) (7308459)
  • A new MemoryCoordinatorPolicyState class was introduced to help policies apply memory-saving directives persistently to consumers, even those registered after a policy is set. (8deb7d8)
  • The internal CriticalUserJourney metrics framework now requires all journeys to be gated by a base::Feature flag. (5407d5a)
  • The TranslateURLFetcher class was refactored into an interface and implementation to improve testability and remove a static global variable. (1e7800a)

Extensions

  • To align with the upcoming W3C browser.test proposal, parameters for chrome.test.assertEq, assertNe, and checkDeepEq have been renamed from expected/actual to the order-agnostic value/other_value. (fbb815d)
  • To improve stability, the EventListenerMap used by the extensions system was changed from base::flat_map to std::map, guaranteeing that iterators are not invalidated during modification. (71c2ebd)
  • A crash in the Omnibox extension provider has been fixed. The crash occurred when an extension provided fewer suggestions than the maximum allowed, causing an out-of-bounds error. (2e27765)

Networking

  • The Network Quality Estimator (NQE) will now capture metrics synchronously when a transaction starts, preventing small or fast responses from being missed and improving throughput accuracy. (7e6c395)
  • The NetworkAsyncRetryOnTooManyConnectionErrors feature has been enabled by default, which should reduce network transaction failures caused by an excessive number of connection errors. (44348b2)
  • The Connection Allowlist enterprise feature was updated to include the policy's source URL in violation reports and to enable reporting to specified endpoints. (49ecd7d) (ec734a0)

Testing & Infrastructure

  • The browser's auto-updater scheduler is now disabled during browser_tests to reduce test flakiness and unnecessary resource usage. (265a116)
  • On macOS, GPU testing has been migrated from x64 to arm64 (Apple M2) builders to better reflect modern hardware. (30a08d7)
  • A build configuration error was fixed for Windows developers by correctly setting is_component_build=false in the windows_developer gn arguments. (11e7171)
  • The syntax for the PollViewProperty observer in the interactive UI testing framework was simplified. (2586877)

2026-04-01

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-01

Overview

This report covers 684 commits on the main branch, with 554 deemed relevant to developers building on Chromium. The day's changes include a significant number of breaking API changes across core content, media, UI, and permissions systems, requiring action from embedders. Key themes include security hardening in drag-and-drop and navigation, the graduation of the SharedWorker API to a stable feature, and ongoing modularization of the codebase. First commit: (9648d93)" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="text-sky-500 hover:text-sky-300 font-mono">((9648d93)), Last commit: (c6fdf14)" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="text-sky-500 hover:text-sky-300 font-mono">((c6fdf14)).

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The core drag-and-drop API has been changed for security reasons. The signature of content::RenderWidgetHostImpl::StartDragging now requires a RenderFrameHost* as a direct parameter. The content::DragDownloadFile constructor signature was also significantly modified. Embedders using these APIs must update their call sites. (988ac5c)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The permission.mojom interface was updated. The return types for RequestPermissions, RevokePermission, and AddPermissionObserver have been changed from a simple status to a PermissionStatusWithDetails struct, breaking clients of this Mojo API. (7052b70) (4e04747) (36e8c6e)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING As a security measure, the Presentation API has been hardened to only permit http:// presentation URLs for localhost. This is a significant behavioral change that prevents its use for discovering arbitrary devices on a local network over insecure HTTP. (52c5e88)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Multiple public C++ API signatures were changed, requiring updates in derived classes or call sites: - media::DataSource::IsStreaming() and WouldTaintOrigin() are now const. - BrowserAutofillManager::FillOrPreviewForm signature was modified. - AutofillClient::GetDeviceAuthenticator is now const. - views::Widget::InitParams no longer has the force_system_menu_for_frameless parameter on Windows. (b156700) (6d25e4a) (109dc40) (7c5af3a)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Several public APIs were removed or renamed: - GetHDRMetadata was removed from the media::AcceleratedVideoDecoder interface. - AccessibilityAnnotationService was renamed to AccessibilityAnnotatorService. - blink::WebString::ContainsOnlyASCII() was renamed to ContainsOnlyAscii(). - The Mojo Rust FFI crate was moved to mojo/public/rust/c_mojo_api, a build-breaking path change. (72e2fc5) (d133671) (e798b84) (84d3aff)

Security

  • Core navigation code was significantly hardened by upgrading numerous DCHECK assertions to CHECKs in NavigationRequest and RenderFrameHostManager. This ensures that internal state inconsistencies will cause a safe crash in release builds, preventing potential security vulnerabilities from corrupted states. (662ea60) (9acb98e) (bf94625)
  • The DevTools protocol was hardened by preventing Input.dispatchDragEvent from bypassing file access restrictions and by restricting experimental page commands to trusted clients only. (59d7444) (d283fa5)
  • A vulnerability was fixed in ui/accessibility where a compromised renderer could bypass bounds clipping on the root web area, potentially allowing it to spoof accessibility focus at arbitrary screen locations. (847f299)
  • The javascript: scheme is now disallowed when setting a home page URL via drag-and-drop to the home button, preventing a potential misuse vector. (b920b51)

APIs and Extensions

  • The SharedWorker API is now enabled by default on all platforms, graduating from its previous experimental status to become a stable web platform feature. (6f4c113)
  • The WebDialogDelegate interface was updated to allow dialogs to specify whether they should process keyboard accelerators. This fixes a regression and improves behavior for components like the file selection dialog. (3d18890)
  • The DICE SIGNIN header parser was updated to support a new hierarchical multi-account format, improving multi-account handling for identity services while maintaining backward compatibility. (d203523)
  • The ExtensionContentVerificationUsesExtensionRoot feature, which alters how extension content integrity is verified, has been enabled by default after a successful experiment. (2aa5824)

Browser UI and Features

  • On macOS, mouse movement event coalescing has been disabled to allow for a higher polling rate. This benefits applications that require high-frequency input, such as games, by providing more responsive mouse input. (80792e0)
  • A significant regression on Windows was fixed where opening URLs from external applications would not respect the current workspace, instead always opening in the last active browser across all workspaces. (0dfe151)
  • Several tab dragging bugs were resolved, including a deadlock related to deferred window visibility and an issue on macOS where dragging the last tab from a fullscreen window would incorrectly move the entire window. (0cdc11e) (9ed9fc9)
  • The logic for theme garbage collection was improved to prevent the incorrect removal of a user's local theme when it is temporarily overridden by a synced theme. (8ab097c)

Media

  • On Windows, a use-after-free bug in MediaFoundationVideoEncodeAccelerator was fixed by decoupling the encoder's lifetime from OS callbacks, improving stability during video encoding. (70f35b0)
  • On macOS, stricter validation was added to the ScreenCaptureKit implementation to prevent unconsented fallback screen capture when using the native picker, enhancing user privacy. (f77c6f1)

Build System and Internals

  • The effort to break down the monolithic //chrome/browser target continued. The offline_pages, plugins, printing, and spellchecker components were moved into their own build files, improving build dependency management and parallelism. (b87be23) (dc7f841) (0790c8f) (0234876)
  • As part of the "Bedrock" UI refactoring initiative, multiple call sites were migrated from the legacy Browser object to modern abstractions like BrowserWindowInterface and GlobalBrowserCollection. (524e16b) (765e765)
  • Test reliability was improved by replacing a flaky RunUntilIdle() call in accessibility tests with a more deterministic wait helper, FlushPendingViewsAXUpdates(). (03b5ae6)

2026-03-31

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-03-31

Overview

This summary covers 710 commits to the Chromium main branch on March 31, 2026, with 549 identified as relevant to developers building on the platform. This period includes a large number of breaking changes to core APIs in UI, services, and extensions. The first relevant commit was (39c2ad8) and the last was (bb2ee6a).

APIs & Services

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public method ContentBrowserClient::UpdateEnabledBlinkRuntimeFeaturesInIsolatedWorker has been removed. Embedders overriding this method to control Blink features in Service Workers must remove their overrides. (b01d62a)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public API for AccountSettingService has been refactored. Feature-specific methods like IsWalletPrivacyContextualSurfacingEnabled() are removed in favor of a generic GetBoolean(const AccountSetting& setting) method. Callers must update to the new generic interface. (19e350b)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public method signature for QueryContextualizer::Contextualize has changed. The callback signature is modified and the session_handle parameter is removed, requiring updates for any embedder using this contextual search component. (a95f201)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The user_gesture boolean parameter has been removed from the RequestPermission and RequestPermissions methods in the public permission.mojom interface. The browser now determines transient activation internally. (5168b89)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public printing API has changed with the removal of PrintedDocument::GetMetafile(). Embedders must now use the new PrintedDocument::HasDocument() and PrintedDocument::GetDocumentData() methods to access the printed document's data. (3ac886f)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public method IsAllowedBy3pcdHeuristicsGrantsSettings has been removed from CookieSettingsBase as part of deleting the obsolete kTpcdHeuristicsGrants feature. Embedders relying on this method will have their builds broken. (8214edf)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The behavior of the public RunIsolatedChrome COM interface on Windows has changed. It now passes non-switch arguments after a -- separator, which can break callers who relied on the previous argument handling. (068662f)
  • The TabStrip API has been enhanced to support closing entire tab groups, in addition to individual tabs. (7cd1de4)
  • A bug in the Web Speech API has been fixed where audio from speechSynthesis could persist after page navigation. Active utterances are now correctly canceled when the primary page changes. (0793f4f)

UI & Views

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public virtual method BrowserWindow::UserChangedTheme() has been removed. Embedders with custom BrowserWindow implementations must now use the new BrowserWindowThemeObserver callback mechanism to listen for theme changes. (a863957)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: Core public drag-and-drop methods in ui/views have been renamed. For example, RunShellDrag is now RunDragDropLoop and OnWidgetDragComplete is now OnWidgetDragDropCompleted. Embedders using views::WidgetObserver or views::MenuDelegate must update to the new names. (e246bab)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The browser_controls_api.mojom interface for WebUI has been updated. Methods like Back(), Forward(), and ReloadFromClick() are no longer fire-and-forget and now return a result via a callback, breaking existing clients. (41af281)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public static method SearchboxHandler::SetupWebUIDataSource has been removed and replaced with SearchboxHandler::GetWebUIDataSourceDict. WebUI implementations using the old helper method will break. (e51273e)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The signature of the ShowContentSettingsBubble method in the public Mojo interface toolbar_ui_api.mojom has been changed. Embedders using this API to build custom toolbars will need to update their code. (023a4ca)
  • The "Send Tab to Self" device picker now displays more granular timestamps, such as "Active 5 minutes ago", for recently used devices. (38f613e)
  • Hover cards are now supported for tab group headers in the horizontal tab strip, providing more information on hover or focus. (e8ce621)
  • PWA uninstallation logic has been improved to properly clean up OS integration resource directories, resolving an issue on Windows where .ico files were left behind. (2a27c7f)

Extensions

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The CWSInfoService has been moved from //chrome/browser/extensions to //extensions/browser. Projects must update the include path from chrome/browser/extensions/cws_info_service.h to extensions/browser/cws_info_service.h to avoid build failures. (d57d493)
  • A critical vulnerability in the chrome.debugger API was fixed. It previously allowed an extension to bypass web_accessible_resources checks and navigate subframes to private extension pages. (5baca06)
  • Extension keybindings are now correctly registered on browser launch, ensuring they are active without requiring manual intervention by the user. (9923357)

Security

  • The networking stack was hardened against HTTP Request Smuggling by fixing an integer overflow in ElementsUploadDataStream that could lead to an incorrect Content-Length header. (4c99d24)
  • The RestrictedCookieManager now reports a bad message and terminates the renderer connection if site_for_cookies or top_frame_origin are incorrect, hardening the API against misuse. (225000a)
  • The client_side_redirect_url in the BeginNavigation IPC message is now properly validated and filtered by the browser process, closing a potential security gap. (59cf813)
  • DevTools security was improved by correctly enforcing file access restrictions for view-source:file:// URLs, which could previously be bypassed. (1f5d394)
  • The core base::PickleIterator has been refactored to use the safer base::SpanReader, enforcing stricter C++ alignment and data handling rules to prevent unsafe buffer usage. (a630cf8) (e98ba17)

Performance

  • A new "Inline Script Cache" feature has been introduced behind the kInlineScriptCache flag. It creates a persistent code cache for inline scripts to improve subsequent page load times. (514b324) (6e5c441)
  • The Performance Manager's logic for Memory Saver has been improved. It now waits for a page to be fully loaded and idle before considering title or favicon changes as user activity, preventing initial load churn from being misclassified. (8da9611)

Build, Testing & Infrastructure

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The legacy bool FromMojom(Input, Output*) signature for Mojo's EnumTraits has been removed. All custom Mojo traits must now use a modern signature that returns the value directly or via std::optional. (a1c3e63)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The kFlagNotEnabled enum value has been removed from the AppUpdateDataReadResult enum in chrome/browser/web_applications/commands/app_update_data_read_command.h. This will cause compilation failures for code that switches on this enum. (3efe506)
  • Integration with Rust has been improved. In developer builds, a Rust panic! will now trigger a LOG(FATAL) in C++, providing more detailed crash reports that include C++, Java, and task runner stack traces. (89012c0)
  • The base/traits_bag.h header has been refactored to use C++20 concepts, providing much clearer compiler errors for invalid trait combinations. (5a5b956)
  • The Mojom wire format is now officially documented, providing a reference for developers working with Mojo IPC. (c289c51)
  • A bug in the Windows accessibility implementation has been fixed to prevent returning uninitialized memory from get_columnHeaderCells and get_rowHeaderCells, which could lead to crashes in COM marshalling. (c81f01b)

2026-03-30

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-03-30

Overview

This summary covers 472 relevant commits out of 631 total for the main branch on March 30, 2026. This digest focuses on changes impacting developers of Chromium-based projects, particularly in APIs, security, networking, and browser UI for Windows and macOS. The day's changes span from the first commit (db61443) to the last, (e14b493).

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING API Removal: The public virtual function GrantCookieAccessDueToHeuristic has been removed from the content::ContentBrowserClient interface. Embedders must remove calls to this now-deleted API to avoid build failures. (ac62164)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Behavioral Change: The ui::Widget class now enforces a CHECK to prevent its own destruction from within its callback stack. Code that previously relied on this incorrect synchronous destruction will now deterministically crash and must be updated to defer destruction. (6b98320)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING API Signature Change: The memory management interface for net::SSLClientSessionCache has been migrated from base::MemoryPressureListener to base::MemoryConsumer. Implementations must be updated to use the new OnUpdateMemoryLimit() and OnReleaseMemory() methods. (f140d6d)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING API Removal: The public method reading_list_side_panel_coordinator() has been removed from the BrowserWindowFeatures interface. Consumers must now access the coordinator via the UnownedUserData::From() pattern. (d0bacbc)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Behavioral Change: The WebAuthn API for extensions now requires host permissions for the specific origin matching the Relying Party (RP) ID, rather than just a subdomain. This stricter validation may break extensions that relied on the previous, more lenient behavior. (ecf43dd)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING API Renaming: A public preference in third_party/blink/public/common/web_preferences/web_preferences.h has been renamed from allow_window_focus_without_user_gesture to allow_unrestricted_window_focus. Embedders setting this preference must update to the new name. (1d4ce65)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Behavioral Change: The content::RenderProcessHostCreationObserver::OnRenderProcessHostCreated callback now fires earlier in the process lifecycle on Windows and macOS. Observers relying on the previous timing, which waited for the process to be fully launched, may break. (d162ee0)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING API Removal: A recent change was reverted, removing the public method WebView::DisconnectWebContentsAccessibility() and its helper ScopedAxDisconnectLock from the ui/views/controls/webview/webview.h header. Any code that adopted this new API will break. (d0b5432)

Security & Privacy

  • Several Use-After-Free (UAF) vulnerabilities were addressed in core components, including DesktopNativeWidgetAura, ServiceWorkerContextCore, and WindowTreeHostPlatform, by improving object lifetime management with weak pointers and auto-reset guards. (95fc202) (d819b46) (cc6431e)
  • A Time-of-Check Time-of-Use (TOCTOU) race condition in the speech recognition flow that could allow a compromised renderer to bypass microphone permissions has been fixed. (714840a)
  • To prevent event hijacking, mouse capture requests are now validated in the browser process to ensure the requesting frame was the target of the most recent MouseDown event. (b885cb0)
  • The DisruptiveNotificationRevocation feature, part of Safety Hub, is now enabled by default on desktop, allowing the browser to automatically revoke permissions from sites that send disruptive notifications. (963d707)
  • A dangling pointer issue in PolicyFetchTracker was resolved by switching to a base::WeakPtr for the Profile object, preventing crashes during shutdown. (3473323)

Browser UI & Features

  • A new keyboard shortcut (Alt+Shift+R on Windows) has been added to open and close Reading Mode. (bd8ce1f)
  • The What's New feature has been finalized, with the WhatsNewDesktopRefresh feature flag removed and the new UI becoming the default experience. (d0bd5f3)
  • A new feature flag, WebUIAvatar, has been added to control the eventual replacement of the native Views-based profile button with a WebUI version. (26d42e6)
  • On macOS 26.4+, a redundant content type registration is now skipped when setting the default browser, preventing a duplicate confirmation dialog from appearing. (b616b00)
  • A bug on Windows that caused maximized, undocked DevTools windows to incorrectly show a resize cursor at the top edge has been fixed. (3af8392)
  • The TabSearchPageHandler was refactored to use the modern TabStripAPI for event listening, improving its architecture. (7c97802)

Networking

  • The NetErrorAutoReloader will no longer automatically reload pages that fail with net::ERR_NETWORK_ACCESS_REVOKED, preventing a potential bypass of network allowlists. (b593efe)
  • For Windows users, Google Credential Provider for Windows (GCPW) now supports configurable verbose logging for the embedded Chrome login flow via the Windows Registry. (e90dddd)
  • The SkipIPCChannelPausingForNonGuests performance feature is now enabled by default on Windows and macOS, affecting how the browser process communicates with new renderer processes. (24c2be5)
  • The testability of NetworkChangeNotifierWin was improved by adding a new constructor to allow injection of a mock SystemDnsConfigChangeNotifier. (54fd8a3)

Extensions & Web Apps

  • The chrome.test.assertEq extension testing API has been updated to use Object.is() for primitive equality checks instead of ===. This is a behavioral change that may require updates to tests, as Object.is(-0, 0) is false. (94def1d)
  • The StreamInfo class was extracted from PdfViewerStreamManager and moved to extensions/browser/mime_handler/ to improve modularity and avoid circular dependencies when handling MIME streams. (aebacdb)
  • Old PWA manifest update code, which was running behind the now-launched "predictable app updating" feature, has been removed. (43b95a9)
  • The webstore_data_fetcher has been moved from //chrome/browser/extensions to //extensions/browser as it no longer has dependencies on //chrome. (e029d19)

API & Refactoring

  • Numerous call sites of chrome::FindLastActive() were updated to use the BrowserWindowInterface* interface instead of the concrete Browser* class, improving abstraction and reducing dependencies. (d3e3cfb)
  • The obsolete //content/browser/tpcd_heuristics directory, related to third-party cookie deprecation heuristics, has been deleted. (037fbcc)
  • The BitmapFetcherService was updated to use the more generic BrowserContext instead of Profile, reducing dependencies on //chrome. (e65d18b)
  • Components of the experimental Tab Organization feature have been removed, including the IDC_START_TAB_ORGANIZATION_REQUEST command and the signin_metrics::AccessPoint::kTabOrganization enum value. (725cd43)

Testing

  • A new test harness (VirtualWallet and DigitalIdentityEnvironment) was introduced in content/browser/digital_credentials/ to support testing for the Digital Credentials API. (4714d48)
  • A new Crossbench benchmark for browser startup performance (browser_startup.crossbench) has been added and scheduled on performance bots. (aa7faad)
  • A new interactive UI test suite was added for the WebUI Toolbar to improve test coverage. (9f893f2)

2026-03-29

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-03-29

Overview

This digest covers 13 commits, with analysis focused on changes relevant to embedders on desktop platforms (Windows/macOS), filtering out test-only, mobile-specific, and internal refactoring changes. A significant breaking API change was identified in the browser UI command set as part of the ongoing 'Bedrock' refactoring. The commits analyzed range from (4d470ec) to (261807f).

Browser API

  • ⚠️ BREAKING As part of the ongoing 'Bedrock' refactoring, numerous public functions in chrome/browser/ui/browser_commands.h and other UI-related headers have changed their signatures. These functions now accept a BrowserWindowInterface* pointer instead of a Browser*. This is a significant breaking change for embedders who directly call these commands, such as chrome::NewTab, chrome::CloseWindow, or chrome::EnterFullscreen. Code will need to be updated to use the new interface. (6fdf39d)

UI

  • A bug fix for Vertical Tabs now triggers a synthetic mouse move event after closing a tab. This change allows for the rapid closing of consecutive tabs, restoring functionality previously available in the horizontal tab strip. (4d470ec)

Testing & Infrastructure

  • The test utility chrome::FindAnyBrowser() has been moved to ui_test_utils::FindAnyBrowser() and reimplemented to use BrowserWindowInterface. This change primarily affects browser test suites and is part of the larger 'Bedrock' initiative to decouple UI logic from the Browser class. (2174233)
  • A large-scale refactoring in the media stack replaced file-level #pragma allow_unsafe_buffers suppressions with more granular UNSAFE_TODO() markers. This is an internal change to improve memory safety enforcement with no expected impact on public APIs. (e0733f4)
  • Routine updates were made to Web Platform Tests (WPT), internal test expectations, and localization string files. (fa4c04c) (4bd518d) (10b5c4b)

2026-03-28

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-03-28

Overview

This digest covers 46 commits from (7709134)" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="text-sky-500 hover:text-sky-300 font-mono">((7709134)) to (7693606)" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="text-sky-500 hover:text-sky-300 font-mono">((7693606)). After filtering for changes relevant to Windows/macOS embedders and browser-level features, this report summarizes 17 key commits. No major breaking changes to stable public APIs were identified in this set.

Browser Features & UI

  • Several changes introduce and refine a new Gemini toolbar button. This includes adding the feature flag (kToolbarGeminiButton) and chrome://flags entry to enable it, implementing the button in the toolbar for both horizontal and vertical tab strips, and applying UX fixes to its size and background color. (00caeeb) (20e8cdc) (262b31e)
  • The Send-Tab-To-Self feature has been enhanced to support sending a tab's full back/forward navigation history, not just the current URL. This involves updates to the core model, sync bridge, and entry definitions to handle the new NavigationHistory data structure. (19645de)
  • Minor UI updates include disabling page zoom for the ContextualTasksUI when it's displayed in a side panel and updating a string on the chrome://settings/defaultBrowser page for better clarity. (4ec5891) (430af7b)

Security

  • The browser has been hardened against potentially misbehaving renderers. One change prevents a renderer from spoofing an OnUnloadACK during the attachment of an inner WebContents (e.g., for a PDF view), which could previously lead to incorrect frame deletion. Another change adds validation to ensure a bundle is associated with the correct renderer process by checking the client_id upon registration. (1ccd3de) (57596dc)
  • To improve stability, reentrancy is now explicitly allowed for ObserverList iterations within the File System Access API implementation, specifically for FileSystemAccessChangeSource and FileSystemAccessObservationGroup. (29a72db)

Core Internals & APIs

  • The base library received several modernizations. base::EnumSet was updated to leverage C++23 features, removing the 64-element limit for constexpr usage and improving performance. Other changes include performance optimizations using emplace and std::move, and the addition of a new 4-argument macro for defining feature parameters. (b518534) (f0a3be2) (a6404ac)
  • The BrowserCollectionObserver public interface in chrome/browser/ui/browser_window/public now explicitly allows reentrancy, making it safer for observers to trigger notifications during their own notification handling. (5c04081)
  • Internal logic for the Variations framework was updated to track entropy usage over time via events rather than a running total. Additionally, the Page Content Annotations component now supports the concatenation of passage embeddings. (80bc68e) (1dcaa2a)

Tooling, Build & Infrastructure

  • New testing utilities have been added to improve test development. A ManifestComponentBuilder was introduced for testing the optimization_guide component. For UKM (URL-keyed metrics) testing, new Gmock matchers like HasMetricWithValue have been added to components/ukm for more descriptive and robust assertions. (13f64e8) (5d91e91)
  • The build system saw several cleanups. The build_with_tflite_lib build option was removed from the Safe Browsing component, as it was universally enabled and the false configuration was unmaintained. Separately, the migration of WebUI ESLint tools to TypeScript has begun, improving the developer tooling for WebUI components. (531c249) (dba0f84)

2026-03-27

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-03-27

Overview

This summary covers 500 relevant commits out of 691 total for the main branch on March 27, 2026, from commit (891da1c)... to (8c4ca14).... The day was marked by numerous breaking changes to core APIs and build system refactoring, impacting embedders in areas like content, UI, sign-in, and base utilities. Notable updates also landed in security, networking, and browser UI, requiring careful review by developers building on Chromium.

API & Core Utilities

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public ContentBrowserClient interface now requires embedders to implement the new pure virtual method IsFullscreenAllowedForUnfocusedWebContents to handle fullscreen requests from unfocused WebContents. (395a125)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The core base::Thread utility was refactored, changing the implementation and potentially the thread-safety contract of public methods like IsRunning() and Stop(). The public method base::CurrentThread::SetTaskRunner was also removed entirely. (440b72b) (031d58e)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public content::WebUIController API has a new virtual method, GetDisplayDisposition, which subclasses must now implement to declare whether the UI is a regular page or an embedded element. (a24f112)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The content::BluetoothAllowedDevices class is now a move-only type, which will break any embedder code that was previously copying instances of this class. (e56dd16)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The pdf.mojom.PdfHost interface was changed, removing SaveUrlAs and replacing it with a new parameter-less SavePdf() method. This is a public Mojo API signature change. (97d9dd9)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The PermissionService::AddPermissionObserver mojom API was updated to accept a PermissionStatusWithDetails struct instead of a PermissionStatus enum, allowing for richer permission state information. (3c88b7a) (eee991e)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The protected virtual method for providing a locale in AimEligibilityService was changed from GetLocale() to GetLocaleImpl(). Subclasses must be updated. (1b9a035)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The ui/ozone/public/ozone_platform.h public interface no longer contains the supports_color_picker_dialog property in OzonePlatform::PlatformProperties. (23eded4)
  • A new re-entrant iteration method, GetReentrantRange(), was added to base::ObserverList to support safe iteration patterns where observer methods might modify the list. (d5a7459)
  • The ui::Layer class now supports backdrop inversion via a new background_inverted property, allowing for more advanced visual effects. (5fd90ca)

Security & Identity

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: Core sign-in API signatures have changed. Methods like AccountsMutator::AddOrUpdateAccount and GaiaAuthFetcher::StartAuthCodeForOAuth2TokenExchange now use a new signin::TokenBindingInfo struct or have new parameters, requiring caller updates. (72fba35) (12d9d6c)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The DidChangeThemeColor and DidChangeBackgroundColor IPCs have been moved from the blink::mojom::LocalFrameHost interface to LocalMainFrameHost, preventing subframes from sending these messages and hardening against compromised renderers. (c4e43fe)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: In the Password Manager component, the PasswordChangeDelegate interface no longer has the OnPasswordFormSubmission method, and an enum value in ManagePasswordsReferrer was renamed from kChromeSettingsHomeOfTransactions to kChromeSettingsAutofillAndPasswords. (0720a68) (2199531)
  • Cookie parsing has been hardened to reject cookies with an empty name and an ambiguous value (e.g., =Foo=Bar) to mitigate potential cookie-shadowing attacks. This is controlled by the DisallowEmptyCookieName feature. (6e5ceda)
  • A security vulnerability in the Extensions API was fixed to correctly enforce webRequestBlocking permissions for non-webview requests, preventing a potential bypass by a compromised renderer. (2619b94)
  • The Sync scheduler logic was improved to attempt a sync cycle even with an expired access token, allowing the network layer to handle the token refresh instead of blocking the scheduler. (e486e98)

Build & Test Infrastructure

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The profile_manager.h header and related files have been moved from //chrome/browser to a new, more granular //chrome/browser/profiles:profile_manager target, which will break downstream projects that depend on the old target. (fa00b2f)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The HarfBuzz dependency directory has been renamed from third_party/harfbuzz-ng to third_party/harfbuzz. This path change will break any build systems or code that directly reference the old path. (2f9f433)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The header webstore_data_fetcher_delegate.h has been relocated from //chrome/browser/extensions to //extensions/browser, causing build failures for projects that include it from its previous location. (9ac1886)
  • The base::TaskEnvironment was updated to ensure mock time advances correctly in tests, fixing an issue where subclasses could have an incorrect time domain. (7506d50)
  • The extensions::test API was updated to enforce strict boolean typing for assertTrue and assertFalse, aligning with the W3C browser.test proposal. (85287c1)

Browser UI & Features

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The memory limit aggregation logic in MemoryCoordinatorPolicyManager was changed from a "minimum-wins" to a multiplicative strategy. This is a major behavioral change for any embedder implementing custom MemoryCoordinatorPolicy classes. (0fd2ab8)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public API LayerTreeHost::CreateSharedMemoryForDroppedFramesUkm() and its corresponding proxy interface have been removed. Embedders using this mechanism for performance monitoring will need to find an alternative. (6c46868)
  • Multiple commits have updated branding from "GPay" to "Google Wallet" across various Autofill and payments dialogs and icons. (ce95852) (da0e34b) (019be57) (9b0f418)
  • A bug on macOS was fixed where dragging the last tab in a fullscreen window would incorrectly move the entire window instead of detaching the tab. (7db44f9)
  • The UI for the Chromium updater on Windows has been updated with a new gradient theme as part of a First Run Experience (FRE) refresh. (dac4b63)
  • The Secure Payment Confirmation (SPC) Capabilities API was enabled for stable release, allowing websites to query for SPC support. (4aa23e7)

Extensions

  • A bug in EventListenerMap was fixed that could cause the removal of event listeners for all service worker instances of an extension when only one was being stopped. The logic now correctly targets only the specific worker's listeners. (05bf9a6)
  • The internal StreamContainer class was moved out of mime_handler_view_guest.h to decouple it from GuestView infrastructure, allowing it to be shared more easily with other components. (8afd2e6)
  • As part of a larger refactoring, the description attribute in the Extension class was moved into a dedicated DescriptionHandler and DescriptionInfo structure. (1791272)

Networking

  • The kMaintainConnectionsOnIpv6TempAddrChange feature was enabled by default to improve connection stability on networks with changing IPv6 temporary addresses. (41a640d)
  • The SpdySession creation logic was updated to call DoDrainSession() asynchronously, preventing potential re-entrancy issues during session initialization. (4073d49)
